content:
────────────────────────
10. Compact Next-Run Task Prompt

Below is the compact “next-run” skeleton prompt that a future AI can use for a narrower job, for example to design the Procedural Generation for Evolving Worlds engine in detail, while honoring this master specification as context.

You can paste this directly into a new run, filling in variables as needed.

[BEGIN NEXT-RUN PROMPT]

[1] OBJECTIVE
Design the Procedural Generation for Evolving Worlds engine in detail, expanding the WORKFLOW-PROCGEN layer of my creative-operating-system so that it can systematically seed, expand, simulate, and archive evolving worlds across conceptual, narrative, and operational dimensions.

[2] BACKGROUND
Treat the existing OS specification stored under $OS_ROOT/specs (especially SYSTEM-, WORKFLOW-PROCGEN-, WORLD-MYTH-*) as canonical context. The goal is to transform the high-level procedures (“seed world; expand entities; simulate interactions; archive deltas; emit prompts for next iterations”) into a concrete, stepwise engine specification that future AIs and scripts can follow. Outputs will be stored in $ARCHIVE_ROOT/specs and $REPO_ROOT/$ORG_ID/os-foundation following the LAYER-DOMAIN-TYPE-ID-VERSION naming grammar.
